skill.name: broken-skill
skill.description: A test fixture for validating skill packager failure handling.
skill.summary: Helps developers test how packaging tools handle incomplete skill metadata.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: The two static weak cryptography findings are false positives. The cited lines contain plain description metadata, and no cryptographic operation, command execution, network request, filesystem access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3, but that line only contains the description text "Broken fixture for packager failure testing." No weak cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain metadata text and contains no cryptographic API, hash function, key handling, or executable instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged agents/interface.yaml line 3, but that line only contains the short description "Missing default prompt on purpose". No weak cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain interface metadata and contains no cryptographic API, hash function, key handling, or executable instruction.

content.value_statement: Skill packaging tools need predictable fixtures for incomplete metadata cases. This skill provides a minimal broken example for testing validation and failure paths.
actual_capabilities: Provides a minimal SKILL.md file with name and description metadata.
Includes interface metadata for a deliberately incomplete skill package.
Declares OpenAI adapter compatibility for metadata adapter testing.
Documents manual activation and inline execution metadata.
Defines trust metadata that forbids remote inline execution.
limitations: It is intentionally incomplete and may fail packaging validation.
It does not provide a usable task workflow or default prompt.
It contains no scripts, examples, or operational automation.
It is mainly useful for tests, not for end users.

use_cases.title: Validate Packager Errors
use_cases.description: Use the fixture to confirm that packaging tools report incomplete skill metadata clearly.

use_cases.title: Test Adapter Fallbacks
use_cases.description: Use the interface metadata to check how adapters handle missing prompt information.

use_cases.title: Document Negative Cases
use_cases.description: Use the small file set as a reference case for failed marketplace ingestion.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this broken-skill fixture and summarize which required metadata fields are present and missing.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Packaging Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this fixture to explain why a skill packager should reject or warn about the package.

prompt_templates.title: Create Test Expectations
prompt_templates.prompt: Write concise test expectations for a packager that processes this incomplete skill fixture.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Adapter Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare how Claude, Codex, and Claude Code should handle this fixture when required prompt metadata is missing.

output_examples.input: Review the fixture for packaging readiness.
output_examples.output: The fixture has a name and description, but it is not ready for normal use because it lacks a default prompt.
The interface metadata targets OpenAI adapter behavior, uses manual activation, and forbids remote inline execution.
Label it as a development testing fixture. It should not be presented as a complete user workflow.

best_practices: Use this fixture only in controlled packaging and adapter tests.
Keep expected validation errors explicit in test documentation.
Pair it with complete skill fixtures when testing positive and negative paths.
anti_patterns: Do not market this fixture as a finished productivity skill.
Do not use it to test runtime behavior because it has no real workflow.
Do not assume missing prompt metadata is safe in production ingestion.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is a small fixture for testing how skill packaging tools handle incomplete metadata.

faq.question: Is it a complete user-facing skill?
faq.answer: No. It is intentionally incomplete and does not define a usable workflow.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No evidence of executable commands was found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Why did static analysis flag weak cryptography?
faq.answer: The flagged lines are plain description metadata. The audit found no cryptographic code there.

faq.question: Can marketplace reviewers publish it safely?
faq.answer: It appears safe from a security perspective, but it should be labeled as a testing fixture.

faq.question: Which tools are listed as supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tool targets.
